$comment: Ch 4 — THE GLASS, scenario 2 (teaching counter-example). Authored from SCENARIO_SPECS_v1.md + SCENARIO_VOICE_v1.md (locked 2026-05-21). Schema mirrors bb-1-baseline.json. The chapter teaches: banks live at the wing; from this angle the math says take the direct shot, not the bank.

meta.title: GEOMETRY SAYS NO.
meta.subtitle: CH 4 — THE GLASS, BANK FROM THE BASELINE
meta.authorIntent: A bank attempted from the baseline. d=4.5, h_r=2.0, h_h=3.05. Textbook answer θ=55°, v=7.49 m/s. Verification: t = 4.5 / (7.49·cos 55°) = 1.047 s; y(1.047) = 2.0 + 7.49·sin(55°)·1.047 − ½·9.8·1.047² = 3.050 m — matches h_h (err 0.000 m). Pedagogical counter-example: the math succeeds because the answer is the direct shot, not the bank. Lesson copy: 'Banks live at the wing. From here, you take the direct shot.'

situation.questionRevealed: Find an angle θ and velocity v that lands the ball through the hoop.

subheadVariants.1: Came up short. From here, the board doesn't save it.
subheadVariants.3+: Short again. Power well below threshold.

subheadVariants.1: Front iron. The board pushed it back.
subheadVariants.3+: Front iron again. Skip the bank.

subheadVariants.1: Back iron. Geometry sent the bounce out.
subheadVariants.2: Still back iron. Cut the velocity.

subheadVariants.1: Long. The reflection added pace, not direction.
subheadVariants.3+: Far overshoot. Power well above threshold.

subheadVariants.1: Air ball. Bank geometry never let it near the rim.
subheadVariants.2: Air ball again. Forget the glass — aim direct.
subheadVariants.3+: Still air ball. Check angle and velocity.

ghostArc.description: Textbook answer θ=55°, v=7.49 m/s. Direct shot — the bank from this angle would push the ball away from the rim, so the geometry's verdict is to skip the glass.

hints.body: Forget the bank. From this spot, glass doesn't help. Solve the direct shot.

Two equations, two unknowns:
  x(t) = v·cos(θ)·t
  y(t) = h_r + v·sin(θ)·t − ½·g·t²
Standard projectile to the rim.
Substitute t = d/(v·cos θ) into y. Try θ near 55°. Lower release, shorter distance — v will be below 8.

workedSteps: Solve y(t) = h_h for v → v ≈ 7.49 m/s.

intro.headline: GEOMETRY SAYS NO.
intro.subhead: From the baseline. The square sends this one away.

subheadByFlavor.SWISH: Skipped the glass. Threaded the rim directly.
subheadByFlavor.GLASS: Geometry argued, the square ignored it.
subheadByFlavor.RIM_DROP: Front iron, then in. The angle paid off.

miss.headline: WRONG ANGLE

diagnosticByCategory.FRONT_RIM: Front iron. The board pushed it back.
diagnosticByCategory.BACK_RIM: Back iron. Geometry sent the bounce out.
diagnosticByCategory.SHORT: Came up short. From here, the board doesn't save it.
diagnosticByCategory.OVERSHOOT: Long. The reflection added pace, not direction.
diagnosticByCategory.AIRBALL: Air ball. Bank geometry never let it near the rim.

bracketHintByCategory.FRONT_RIM: Geometry: skip the bank. Aim direct, θ near 55°.
bracketHintByCategory.BACK_RIM: Geometry: direct shot, cut v by ~0.5.
bracketHintByCategory.SHORT: Geometry: direct shot, θ near 55°, push v near 7.5.
bracketHintByCategory.OVERSHOOT: Geometry: direct shot, cut v by ~0.8.
bracketHintByCategory.AIRBALL: Geometry: forget the glass. θ near 55°, v near 7.5.

miss.afterAllHintsCopy: All hints revealed. The math is on the table.
